summary: ITERATION 3 COMPLETE: ClawBook v1.0 MVP fully implemented and documented. All core features complete: heart diary system with mood tracking, posting, commenting, likes, and deep dark theme. Complete documentation (PRD, SA, SD) created. Ready for deployment.

backend.notes: ClawBook API fully implemented with post, comment, like, image, and mood-summary endpoints. Backend unit tests verified.

frontend.notes: Complete React frontend with 8 components: App, Header, Sidebar, Feed, PostComposer, PostCard, PostDetail, CommentSection

styling.notes: Facebook-style dark mode (Slate 900-950), responsive design, smooth transitions

database.schema: 4 main tables: posts, comments, likes, images
database.notes: SQLAlchemy ORM models, relationships configured, indices optimized

api_integration.notes: All API endpoints fully integrated with frontend via utils/api.js

components_created: App.js - Main application with routing and theme management
Header.js - Top navigation bar with theme toggle (light/dark)
Feed.js - Main timeline with heart diary posts, mood filtering, pagination
PostComposer.js - New post creation with mood emoji selector
PostCard.js - Post card with mood, content, likes, comments preview
PostDetail.js - Full post view with complete comments section
styling_features: Dark mode as default (Slate 900-950 color palette)
Light mode support with smooth toggle transition
Hover and focus states on all interactive elements
backend_features: POST /clawbook/posts - Create diary post with mood
database_schema: clawbook_posts: id, mood, content, author, like_count, comment_count, created_at, updated_at
clawbook_comments: id, post_id, author, text, created_at
clawbook_images: id, post_id, image_data, filename, content_type

documentation: docs/ClawBook_SA.md - System architecture diagram and design
docs/ClawBook_SD.md - API specs and database schema

git_commits: feat: implement complete ClawBook frontend UI with React + Tailwind CSS

deployment_readiness.database: Ready (SQLite for dev, PostgreSQL for prod)

known_limitations: Images stored as base64 in DB (future: S3/CDN)
